description: Per-biomarker surface variations for the NCCN v1.2026 Tier 1 NSCLC biomarker panel. Weights are probabilities (floats summing to 1.0 per distribution, validated at load time).
_changelog: v1.1: Normalized population keys across all biomarkers using composable multi-axis schema with documented cascade.
v1.1: Added adenocarcinoma and squamous as required terminal fallback keys for every biomarker.
v1.1: Unified variant prevalence field to prevalence_within_biomarker (dropped _given_positive suffix).
v1.1: Extracted negative renderings for expression biomarkers (PD-L1, TMB) into separate negative_forms block; removed degenerate 0.0-prevalence variants.
v1.1: Extracted PD-L1 IHC clones into independent clone_attribution block (not a variant).
v1.1: Added render_constraints.require_biomarker_name_forms to fusion unspecified_fusion variants to couple name-form sampling.

_changelog.change: Strip gene-name prefix from variant realizations
_changelog.reason: Frame {gene} {variant} was producing duplicated gene names (e.g. "KRAS KRAS G12C"). Bug 3b affected ~2% of generated records.

_changelog.change: Extend gene-prefix strip to hyphen-boundary and negative_forms
_changelog.reason: Initial strip missed "TMB-high"/"TMB-low" (hyphen boundary) and TMB negative_forms ("TMB {value} mut/Mb"). Bug 3b remaining rate 1.71% → expected near zero.

_schema_template.description: Reference structure — every biomarker entry follows this shape

name_forms.description: Surface variations of how the biomarker name appears
name_forms.realizations: dict of form_id -> actual string rendered

variants.description: Sub-variants (e.g., L858R within EGFR). prevalence_within_biomarker is conditional probability given the biomarker has a meaningful result to report. For mutations this means positive; for expression biomarkers this means any non-negative meaningful result. Sums to 1.0 across variants.

<variant_id>.category: string label for grouping similar variants
<variant_id>.measurement_range: [min, max] — only for expression biomarkers with {value} placeholders

render_constraints.require_biomarker_name_forms: optional list — if present, biomarker-level name_form sampling is restricted to this subset (weights renormalized within subset)

negative_forms.description: ONLY for expression biomarkers (PD-L1, TMB). Surface variations for rendering negative results. Rendered when status=negative instead of sampling from variants.

clone_attribution.description: ONLY for IHC-based biomarkers (PD-L1). Independent sampling dimension for antibody clone attribution. attachment_probability controls Bernoulli roll for whether to attach; variations/realizations are the clone options.

status_distribution_by_population.description: Prior probability of each status outcome, per simulated patient subpopulation. Keys follow composable multi-axis schema (see config_architecture.md Section 2.3). MUST include 'adenocarcinoma' and 'squamous' as terminal fallbacks.

preferred_methods.description: Biomarker-specific weights for test methods. Keys MUST be a subset of common.test_methods.variations. Realizations are looked up in common.test_methods.realizations at render time (no local realizations dict).

exon_20_insertion.notes: Heterogeneous — 60+ unique variants; many not detectable by PCR

T790M.notes: Resistance mutation; rarely primary; common post 1st/2nd gen TKI

KRAS.canonical_name: Kirsten Rat Sarcoma Viral Oncogene Homolog

G12C.notes: Only FDA-approved targetable KRAS variant as of 2026 (sotorasib, adagrasib)

ALK.notes: Fusion/rearrangement biomarker. Primary alteration type is rearrangement, not point mutation. Variant list is fusion partners.

EML4_ALK.notes: Dominant fusion partner. Multiple structural variants (V1, V2, V3, V5).

unspecified_fusion.notes: Reports often omit specific fusion partner. Renderer must force biomarker name_form that carries fusion/rearrangement signal.
Renderer must force biomarker name_form that carries fusion/rearrangement signal.

V600E.notes: Only FDA-approved actionable BRAF variant in NSCLC (dabrafenib + trametinib)

MET.notes: Three distinct actionable alteration types: exon 14 skipping, de novo amplification, acquired amplification. Variants categorize these.

exon_14_skipping.notes: Splice site mutations causing exon 14 loss; actionable with capmatinib/tepotinib

amplification_de_novo.notes: De novo; distinguished from acquired resistance amplification

amplification_acquired.notes: Acquired post-EGFR TKI (typically osimertinib); most common osimertinib resistance mechanism

KIF5B_RET.notes: Most common RET fusion partner in NSCLC

unspecified_fusion.notes: Renderer must force biomarker name_form that carries fusion/rearrangement signal.

ERBB2.notes: Three alteration categories: mutations (mostly exon 20 insertions), amplification, overexpression (IHC). Variants categorize these.

exon_20_insertion.notes: A775_G776insYVMA is most common; actionable with T-DXd and sevabertinib

overexpression_IHC.notes: Measured by IHC; different from amplification

other_mutation.notes: L755S, V842I, G660D — less common mutations

NTRK.notes: Pan-NTRK biomarker collapsing NTRK1/2/3. In practice reports often say 'NTRK fusion' without specifying subtype.

unspecified_NTRK_fusion.notes: Renderer must force biomarker name_form that carries fusion/rearrangement signal.

PD-L1.notes: Expression-level biomarker. Variants represent positive scoring categories. negative_forms handles negative renderings separately. clone_attribution is an independent dimension.

CPS.notes: Less common in NSCLC; more in gastric/head and neck

negative_forms.description: Rendered when status=negative. Separate from variants because PD-L1 negative is a distinct expression category, not a conditional-on-positive variant.

clone_attribution.description: IHC antibody clone attribution. Sampled INDEPENDENTLY of the TPS/CPS variant: first roll a Bernoulli with attachment_probability to decide whether to attach a clone at all, then sample a clone from variations if attached.

TMB.notes: Quantitative panel-derived metric. Measurement is mutations per megabase. Variants represent positive categories; negative_forms handles low-TMB renderings.

negative_forms.description: Rendered when status=negative. TMB-low is a distinct low-expression category, not a variant.
